+3906     if not controller or not _stack_engine:
+3907         return
+3908 
+3909     client = controller.client
+3910     ws_mgr = WebSocketManager()
+3911 
+3912     for i in range(total_frames):
+3913         if not _stack_engine._running or _stack_engine._cancelled:
+3914             break
+3915 
+3916         try:
+3917             pic_resp = client.get("/Streaming/channels/1/picture")
+3918             if pic_resp.status_code != 200:
+3919                 continue
+3920             arr = np.frombuffer(pic_resp.raw_bytes if hasattr(pic_resp, 'raw_bytes') else b'', np.uint8)
+3921             bgr = cv2.imdecode(arr, cv2.IMREAD_COLOR)
+3922             if bgr is None:
+3923                 continue
+3924 
+3925             result = _stack_engine.add_frame(bgr)
+3926             if result and result.get("preview_jpg_base64"):
+3927                 # жҺЁйҖҒйў„и§ҲеҲ° WebSocket
+3928                 await ws_mgr.broadcast_json({
+3929                     "type": "stack_preview",
+3930                     "frame": result["index"],
+3931                     "total": total_frames,
+3932                     "image": result["preview_jpg_base64"],
+3933                 })

+3935             continue

+3937         await asyncio.sleep(exposure_ms / 1000.0)

+3942         await ws_mgr.broadcast_json({
+3943             "type": "stack_complete",
+3944             **final,
+3945         })

## v8.10 жӣҙж–°пјҲ2026-07-02пјү

### ж–°еўһеҠҹиғҪи®ҫи®Ў

2. **жҳҹзӮ№еҜ№йҪҗеҸ еҠ **: ISAPIжҲӘеӣҫеҫӘзҺҜ вҶ’ SEPжҳҹзӮ№жЈҖжөӢ вҶ’ дёүи§’еҢ№й…ҚеҜ№йҪҗ вҶ’ Sigma ClipеҸ еҠ  вҶ’ SNRжҸҗеҚҮ

## Project description

Python and C library for Source Extraction and Photometry.

https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sep
https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sep

https://sep-pjw.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
http://dx.doi.org/10.21105/joss.00058

"... [it's] an SEP: Somebody Else's Problem."
"Oh, good. I can relax then."

## #user-content-sep-sep-pjw-and-package-namesSEP, SEP-PJW, and Package Names

sep was originally released by Kyle Barbary, at
[kbarbary/sep](https://github.com/kbarbary/sep) (sep<=1.2.1). For a
brief period, the package was maintained by Peter Watson, under the
sep-pjw package name, at
[PJ-Watson/sep-pjw](https://github.com/PJ-Watson/sep-pjw) and
[PyPI/sep-pjw](https://pypi.org/project/sep-pjw/)
(1.3.0<=sep-pjw<=1.3.8). Both of these repositories will be archived,
and future development will take place at
[sep-developers/sep](https://github.com/sep-developers/sep)
(sep>=1.4.0). Note that there may be some incompatibilities between
sep==1.2.1 and sep==1.4.0 when using the C-API directly (to fix
an indexing bug arising with large arrays) - all changes are documented
[here](https://sep-pjw.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelogs/changes_to_c_api.html).

## #user-content-aboutAbout

[Source Extractor](http://www.astromatic.net/software/sextractor)
(Bertin & Arnouts 1996) is a widely used
command-line program for segmentation and analysis of astronomical
images. It reads in FITS format files, performs a configurable series
of tasks, including background estimation, source detection,
deblending and a wide array of source measurements, and finally
outputs a FITS format catalog file.

While Source Extractor is highly useful, the fact that it can only be
used as an executable can limit its applicability or lead to awkward
workflows. There is often a desire to have programmatic access to
perform one or more of the above tasks on in-memory images as part of
a larger custom analysis.

SEP makes the core algorithms of Source Extractor available as a
library of stand-alone functions and classes. These operate directly
on in-memory arrays (no FITS files or configuration files). The code
is derived from the Source Extractor code base (written in C) and aims
to produce results compatible with Source Extractor whenever possible.
SEP consists of a C library with no dependencies outside the standard
library, and a Python module that wraps the C library in a Pythonic
API. The Python wrapper operates on NumPy arrays with NumPy as its
only dependency. See below for language-specfic build and usage
instructions.

## #user-content-pythonPython

Documentation: [http://sep.readthedocs.io](http://sep.readthedocs.io)

Requirements:

- Python 3.9+

- numpy 1.23+

Install release version:

SEP can be installed with [pip](https://pip.pypa.io):

python -m pip install sep

If you get an error about permissions, you are probably using your
system Python. In this case, we recommend using [pip's "user
install"](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/user_guide/#user-installs)
option to install sep into your user directory:

python -m pip install --user sep

Do not install sep or other third-party Python packages using
sudo unless you are fully aware of the risks.

Install development version:

Building the development version (from github) requires Cython.
Build and install in the usual place:

python -m pip install --editable .

Run tests: Tests require the [pytest](http://pytest.org) Python
package. To run the tests, execute ./test.py in the top-level
directory. Some tests require a FITS reader (either fitsio or astropy)
and will be skipped if neither is present.

## #user-content-c-libraryC Library

Note: The build process only works on Linux and OS X.

CMake: To build using CMake, enter these commands:

cd sep
mkdir -p build
cd build
cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr ../

and follow next steps from the build folder

Build: To build the C library from source:

make

Run tests:

make test

Install The static library and header can be installed with

make install
make PREFIX=/path/to/prefix install

This will install the shared and static library in /path/to/
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="9b00b1c41ef4f779">>>"

## Project description

Python and C library for Source Extraction and Photometry.

https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sep
https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sep

https://sep-pjw.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
http://dx.doi.org/10.21105/joss.00058

"... [it's] an SEP: Somebody Else's Problem."
"Oh, good. I can relax then."

## #user-content-sep-sep-pjw-and-package-namesSEP, SEP-PJW, and Package Names

sep was originally released by Kyle Barbary, at
[kbarbary/sep](https://github.com/kbarbary/sep) (sep<=1.2.1). For a
brief period, the package was maintained by Peter Watson, under the
sep-pjw package name, at
[PJ-Watson/sep-pjw](https://github.com/PJ-Watson/sep-pjw) and
[PyPI/sep-pjw](https://pypi.org/project/sep-pjw/)
(1.3.0<=sep-pjw<=1.3.8). Both of these repositories will be archived,
and future development will take place at
[sep-developers/sep](https://github.com/sep-developers/sep)
(sep>=1.4.0). Note that there may be some incompatibilities between
sep==1.2.1 and sep==1.4.0 when using the C-API directly (to fix
an indexing bug arising with large arrays) - all changes are documented
[here](https://sep-pjw.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelogs/changes_to_c_api.html).

## #user-content-aboutAbout

[Source Extractor](http://www.astromatic.net/software/sextractor)
(Bertin & Arnouts 1996) is a widely used
command-line program for segmentation and analysis of astronomical
images. It reads in FITS format files, performs a configurable series
of tasks, including background estimation, source detection,
deblending and a wide array of source measurements, and finally
outputs a FITS format catalog file.

While Source Extractor is highly useful, the fact that it can only be
used as an executable can limit its applicability or lead to awkward
workflows. There is often a desire to have programmatic access to
perform one or more of the above tasks on in-memory images as part of
a larger custom analysis.

SEP makes the core algorithms of Source Extractor available as a
library of stand-alone functions and classes. These operate directly
on in-memory arrays (no FITS files or configuration files). The code
is derived from the Source Extractor code base (written in C) and aims
to produce results compatible with Source Extractor whenever possible.
SEP consists of a C library with no dependencies outside the standard
library, and a Python module that wraps the C library in a Pythonic
API. The Python wrapper operates on NumPy arrays with NumPy as its
only dependency. See below for language-specfic build and usage
instructions.

## #user-content-pythonPython

Documentation: [http://sep.readthedocs.io](http://sep.readthedocs.io)

Requirements:

- Python 3.9+

- numpy 1.23+

Install release version:

SEP can be installed with [pip](https://pip.pypa.io):

python -m pip install sep

If you get an error about permissions, you are probably using your
system Python. In this case, we recommend using [pip's "user
install"](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/user_guide/#user-installs)
option to install sep into your user directory:

python -m pip install --user sep

Do not install sep or other third-party Python packages using
sudo unless you are fully aware of the risks.

Install development version:

Building the development version (from github) requires Cython.
Build and install in the usual place:

python -m pip install --editable .

Run tests: Tests require the [pytest](http://pytest.org) Python
package. To run the tests, execute ./test.py in the top-level
directory. Some tests require a FITS reader (either fitsio or astropy)
and will be skipped if neither is present.

## #user-content-c-libraryC Library

Note: The build process only works on Linux and OS X.

CMake: To build using CMake, enter these commands:

cd sep
mkdir -p build
cd build
cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr ../

and follow next steps from the build folder

Build: To build the C library from source:

make

Run tests:

make test

Install The static library and header can be installed with

make install
make PREFIX=/path/to/prefix install

This will install the shared and static library in /path/to/

content.thinking:
The PyPI page shows SEP only has `.tar.gz` source distributions, no prebuilt wheels. This means it always needs C compilation. The latest is sep-1.4.1 which only has source distribution. No wheels for any platform.

Python 3.14 is very new. The issue is that SEP is a C extension and needs a C compiler to build. On Windows, that means Visual Studio Build Tools.

Let me check:
1. If there's a way to get SEP working on Python 3.14 on Windows
2. Alternatives to SEP

Let me check what wheels are actually available and what Python versions are supported. The sep-1.4.1 was released as source-only. Let me also check if there's a conda-forge version.

Actually, looking more carefully, the issue is that SEP is only distributed as source (tar.gz), never as prebuilt wheels. So it always requires a C compiler. On Windows, this needs Visual C++ Build Tools.

Let me check alternatives and also see if we can install C build tools.

Alternatives to SEP:
1. `photutils` (astropy) - has source detection with `DAOStarFinder`, `IRAFStarFinder` - pure Python, works with any Python version
2. `astropy.stats.sigma_clip` - already have scipy, can use for sigma clipping
3. `cv2.SimpleBlobDetector` - OpenCV built-in blob detector
4. Install Visual Studio Build Tools to compile SEP

Let me check if Visual C++ is available on this system.

Actually, let me reconsider the approach. The real question is: what's the best star detection method available?

SEP is the gold standard but needs compilation. `photutils` from astropy has `DAOStarFinder` which is a solid pure-Python alternative. It's widely used in the astronomy community.

Let me give the boss a clear summary and then implement the best available solution.

"""AstroHub v8.10 - жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹеҲҶжһҗ

"""
import cv2
import numpy as np


def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """Gray World зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®—гҖӮ

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
    if g_mean < 1:
        g_mean = 1  # йҳІйҷӨйӣ¶
    red_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
    blue_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
    return {
        "red_gain": red_gain,
        "blue_gain": blue_gain,
        "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1),
        "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1),
        "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
    }

def _to_jpg_base64(bgr: np.ndarray, quality: int = 85) -> str:
    """BGR numpy array вҶ’ JPEG base64 string."""
    _, buf = cv2.imencode('.jpg', bgr, [cv2.IMWRITE_JPEG_QUALITY, quality])
    return base64.b64encode(buf).decode('utf-8')


def _sigma_clip_mean(stack: np.ndarray, sigma: float = 2.0) -> np.ndarray:
    """Sigma Clip еқҮеҖјеҸ еҠ гҖӮNГ—HГ—WГ—C вҶ’ HГ—WГ—C"""
    mean = np.mean(stack, axis=0)
    std = np.std(stack, axis=0)
    lower = mean - sigma * std
    upper = mean + sigma * std
    valid = (stack >= lower) & (stack <= upper)
    result = np.sum(stack * valid, axis=0) / np.maximum(np.sum(valid, axis=0), 1)
    return result

# 1. зҷҪе№іиЎЎ
bgr = np.random.randint(0, 255, (200, 200, 3), dtype=np.uint8)

print(f'[OK] whitebalance: R={wb["red_gain"]} B={wb["blue_gain"]}')

# 2. еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ
af = ContrastAF()
cmd = af.step(bgr)
assert cmd['action'] in ('focus_far', 'focus_near', 'focus_stop')

# 6. з«ҜзӮ№еӯҳеңЁжҖ§жЈҖжҹҘ
import ast
router_code = open('src/api/router.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
tree = ast.parse(router_code)
routes = [n for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and
          hasattr(n.func, 'attr') and n.func.attr in ('get', 'post', 'put', 'delete')]
vision_routes = [r for r in routes if any(
    isinstance(kw.value, ast.Str) and 'vision' in kw.value.s for kw in r.keywords
    if kw.arg == 'path'
)]
print(f'[OK] vision routes: {len(vision_routes)}')
for r in vision_routes:
    path = next(kw.value.s for kw in r.keywords if kw.arg == 'path')
    print(f'  {path}')

content.text: жҲӘеӣҫжӢҝеҲ°дәҶгҖӮзҺ°еңЁеҶҷ E2E и„ҡжң¬пјҢз”Ёзңҹе®һеӣҫзүҮжЁЎжӢҹжЎҶйҖүвҶ’еҲҶжһҗвҶ’еә”з”ЁгҖӮ

arguments.content:
"""v8.10 E2E - зҷҪе№іиЎЎ + еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ з«ҜеҲ°з«ҜжөӢиҜ•
жЁЎжӢҹ: жҲӘеӣҫ вҶ’ иЈҒеүӘеҢәеҹҹ вҶ’ POSTеҲҶжһҗ вҶ’ йӘҢиҜҒз»“жһң
"""
import sys
import base64
import cv2
import numpy as np
import requests
import json
from pathlib import Path

BASE = "http://127.0.0.1:10280/api/v1"
DEVICE = "192.168.5.72"
passed = 0
failed = 0
results = []

def check(name, condition, detail=""):
    global passed, failed
    if condition:
        passed += 1
        results.append(f"  PASS {name}")
        print(f"  [PASS] {name}{' - ' + detail if detail else ''}")
    else:
        failed += 1
        results.append(f"  FAIL {name}: {detail}")
        print(f"  [FAIL] {name}: {detail}")

def region_b64_from_file(image_path, x, y, w, h):
    """д»ҺжҲӘеӣҫж–Үд»¶иЈҒеүӘеҢәеҹҹ вҶ’ base64"""
    img = cv2.imread(image_path)
    if img is None:
        return None
    crop = img[y:y+h, x:x+w]
    _, buf = cv2.imencode('.jpg', crop, [cv2.IMWRITE_JPEG_QUALITY, 85])
    return base64.b64encode(buf).decode('utf-8')

print("=" * 60)
print("v8.10 E2E: зҷҪе№іиЎЎ + еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ")
print("=" * 60)

# в”Җв”Җ Step 1: жҲӘеӣҫиҺ·еҸ–зңҹе®һз”»йқў в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[1] жҲӘеӣҫиҺ·еҸ–зңҹе®һз”»йқў...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/ptz/{DEVICE}/capture", timeout=30)
data = r.json()
check("жҲӘеӣҫжҲҗеҠҹ", data.get("success"), data.get("message", ""))
image_path = data.get("data", {}).get("image_path", "")
check("жңүж–Үд»¶и·Ҝеҫ„", bool(image_path), image_path)

if not image_path or not Path(image_path).exists():
    print(f"  [FATAL] жҲӘеӣҫж–Үд»¶дёҚеӯҳеңЁ: {image_path}")
    sys.exit(1)

# в”Җв”Җ Step 2: иҜ»еҸ–еӣҫзүҮе№¶иЈҒеүӘдёӯеҝғеҢәеҹҹ в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[2] иҜ»еҸ–еӣҫзүҮе№¶иЈҒеүӘдёӯеҝғеҢәеҹҹ...")
img = cv2.imread(image_path)
check("еӣҫзүҮеҸҜиҜ»", img is not None, f"shape={img.shape if img is not None else 'None'}")

h, w = img.shape[:2]
cx, cy = w // 2, h // 2
# иЈҒеүӘдёӯеӨ® 200x200 еҢәеҹҹ
rw, rh = 200, 200
rx, ry = cx - rw // 2, cy - rh // 2
region_b64 = region_b64_from_file(image_path, rx, ry, rw, rh)
check("еҢәеҹҹиЈҒеүӘ+base64зј–з Ғ", region_b64 is not None and len(region_b64) > 0,
      f"length={len(region_b64) if region_b64 else 0}")
check("base64д»Ҙ/9jејҖеӨҙ(JPEG)", region_b64.startswith("/9j/") if region_b64 else False)

# в”Җв”Җ Step 3: POST /vision/region-analyze (whitebalance) в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[3] POST /vision/region-analyze (whitebalance)...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/region-analyze", json={
    "image_base64": region_b64,
    "action": "whitebalance"
}, timeout=30)
wb_data = r.json()
check("HTTP 200", r.status_code == 200, f"status={r.status_code}")
check("success=true", wb_data.get("success"), wb_data.get("message", ""))
check("иҝ”еӣһ whitebalance ж•°жҚ®", "whitebalance" in wb_data,
      str(wb_data.get("whitebalance", "")))
if "whitebalance" in wb_data:
    wb = wb_data["whitebalance"]
    check("red_gain жҳҜж•ҙж•°", isinstance(wb.get("red_gain"), (int, float)))
    check("blue_gain жҳҜж•ҙж•°", isinstance(wb.get("blue_gain"), (int, float)))
    check("r_mean жңүеҖј", isinstance(wb.get("r_mean"), (int, float)))
    check("g_mean жңүеҖј", isinstance(wb.get("g_mean"), (int, float)))
    check("b_mean жңүеҖј", isinstance(wb.get("b_mean"), (int, float)))
    print(f"    вҶ’ R={wb.get('red_gain')} B={wb.get('blue_gain')} "
          f"(Rmean={wb.get('r_mean')} Gmean={wb.get('g_mean')} Bmean={wb.get('b_mean')})")

# в”Җв”Җ Step 4: POST /vision/region-analyze (focus) в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[4] POST /vision/region-analyze (focus)...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/region-analyze", json={
    "image_base64": region_b64,
    "action": "focus"
}, timeout=30)
focus_data = r.json()
check("HTTP 200", r.status_code == 200, f"status={r.status_code}")
check("success=true", focus_data.get("success"), focus_data.get("message", ""))
check("иҝ”еӣһ contrast еҖј", "contrast" in focus_data,
      str(focus_data.get("contrast", "")))
if "contrast" in focus_data:
    check("contrast > 0", focus_data["contrast"] > 0,
          f"contrast={focus_data['contrast']:.1f}")
    print(f"    вҶ’ contrast={focus_data['contrast']:.1f}")

# в”Җв”Җ Step 5: POST /vision/region-analyze (both) в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[5] POST /vision/region-analyze (both)...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/region-analyze", json={
    "image_base64": region_b64,
    "action": "both"
}, timeout=30)
both_data = r.json()
check("HTTP 200", r.status_code == 200)
check("success=true", both_data.get("success"))
check("еҗҢж—¶иҝ”еӣһ whitebalance", "whitebalance" in both_data)
check("еҗҢж—¶иҝ”еӣһ contrast", "contrast" in both_data)

# в”Җв”Җ Step 6: POST /vision/whitebalance-apply в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[6] POST /vision/whitebalance-apply...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/whitebalance-apply", json={
    "device_id": DEVICE,
    "red_gain": 100,
    "blue_gain": 100
}, timeout=30)
wb_apply = r.json()
check("HTTP 200", r.status_code == 200)
check("жңүиҝ”еӣһж¶ҲжҒҜ", "message" in wb_apply, str(wb_apply)[:100])
print(f"    вҶ’ {wb_apply.get('message', wb_apply.get('success'))}")

# в”Җв”Җ Step 7: POST /vision/focus-search в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[7] POST /vision/focus-search...")
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/focus-search", json={
    "device_id": DEVICE,
    "image_base64": region_b64
}, timeout=120)
fs_data = r.json()
check("HTTP 200", r.status_code == 200)
check("жңүиҝ”еӣһж¶ҲжҒҜ", "message" in fs_data or "success" in fs_data, str(fs_data)[:200])
print(f"    вҶ’ {json.dumps(fs_data, ensure_ascii=False)[:200]}")

# в”Җв”Җ Step 8: иҫ№з•ҢжөӢиҜ• в”Җв”Җ
print("\n[8] иҫ№з•ҢжөӢиҜ•...")
# з©әbase64
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/region-analyze", json={
    "image_base64": "",
    "action": "both"
}, timeout=30)
check("з©әbase64иҝ”еӣһйқһ200", r.status_code != 500)
print(f"    вҶ’ з©әbase64: status={r.status_code} msg={r.json().get('message', '')[:80]}")

# ж— и®ҫеӨҮID
r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/vision/focus-search", json={
    "device_id": "0.0.0.0",
    "image_base64": region_b64
}, timeout=30)
check("ж— ж•Ҳи®ҫеӨҮдёҚеҙ©жәғ", r.status_code != 500)
print(f"    вҶ’ ж— ж•Ҳи®ҫеӨҮ: status={r.status_code} msg={r.json().get('message', '')[:80]}")

# в”Җв”Җ з»“жһңжұҮжҖ» в”Җв”Җ
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print(f"  з»“жһң: {passed} PASS / {failed} FAIL / {passed+failed} TOTAL")
if failed == 0:
    print("  вң… ALL PASS")
else:
    print("  вқҢ FAILURES DETECTED")
print("=" * 60)

"""v8.10 E2E: Playwright зңҹе®һ UI жөӢиҜ• - зҷҪе№іиЎЎ + еҜ№з„Ұ
жЁЎжӢҹ: йЎөйқўеҠ иҪҪ вҶ’ еҲҮиҮӘеҠЁзҷҪе№іиЎЎ вҶ’ жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎ вҶ’ жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„Ұ вҶ’ йӘҢиҜҒз»“жһң
"""
import time, sys
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

URL = "http://127.0.0.1:10280"
passed = 0
failed = 0

def check(name, condition, detail=""):
    global passed, failed
    if condition:
        passed += 1
        print(f"  [PASS] {name}{' - ' + detail if detail else ''}")
    else:
        failed += 1
        print(f"  [FAIL] {name}: {detail}")

def run():
    global passed, failed
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=False)
        page = browser.new_page(viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080})

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 1: жү“ејҖжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ° в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[1] жү“ејҖжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°...")
        page.goto(URL, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", timeout=15000)
        check("йЎөйқўеҠ иҪҪ", True, "жҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°еҸҜи§Ғ")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 2: зӯүеҫ…и®ҫеӨҮиҝһжҺҘ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[2] зӯүеҫ…и®ҫеӨҮиҝһжҺҘ...")
        # еҲҮжҚўеҲ°и®ҫеӨҮйЎөйқўиҝһжҺҘ
        page.click("[data-page='devices']")
        time.sleep(1)
        # жүҫејҖе…іжҢүй’®иҝһжҺҘ
        try:
            switch = page.query_selector("#device-240f9b764193 .device-switch")
            if switch:
                is_checked = switch.is_checked() if hasattr(switch, 'is_checked') else switch.get_attribute("checked")
                if not is_checked:
                    switch.click()
                    time.sleep(2)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  [INFO] иҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮејӮеёё: {e}")

        # еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°
        page.click("[data-page='console']")
        time.sleep(3)
        check("еӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°", True)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 3: еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸ вҶ’ иҮӘеҠЁ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[3] еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸ вҶ’ иҮӘеҠЁ...")
        try:
            page.wait_for_selector("#wbMode", timeout=10000)
            # зӯүдёӢжӢүйҖүйЎ№еҠ иҪҪ
            time.sleep(2)
            wb_select = page.query_selector("#wbMode")
            options = wb_select.evaluate("el => Array.from(el.options).map(o => ({text: o.text, value: o.value}))")
            print(f"    зҷҪе№іиЎЎйҖүйЎ№: {options}")
            # йҖү"иҮӘеҠЁ(е®ҳж–№)" жҲ– "auto"
            for opt in options:
                if "иҮӘеҠЁ" in opt["text"] or "auto" in opt["value"]:
                    page.select_option("#wbMode", opt["value"])
                    check(f"еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸ вҶ’ {opt['text']}", True)
                    time.sleep(2)
                    break
            else:
                check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸеҲҮжҚў", False, f"ж— иҮӘеҠЁйҖүйЎ№: {options}")
        except Exception as e:
            check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸеҲҮжҚў", False, str(e))

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 4: зӮ№еҮ»зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’® вҶ’ иҝӣе…ҘжЎҶйҖүжЁЎејҸ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[4] зӮ№еҮ»зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®...")
        btn_wb = page.query_selector("#btnRegionWB")
        check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®еӯҳеңЁ", btn_wb is not None)
        btn_wb.click()
        time.sleep(1)

        # йӘҢиҜҒ overlay еҮәзҺ°
        overlay = page.query_selector("#regionOverlay")
        overlay_display = overlay.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).display")
        check("overlayжҳҫзӨә", overlay_display != "none", f"display={overlay_display}")
        check("жҢүй’®зӮ№дә®", "btn-primary" in btn_wb.get_attribute("class") or False)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 5: жЎҶйҖүи§Ҷйў‘дёӯеҝғеҢәеҹҹ(зҷҪе№іиЎЎ) в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[5] жЎҶйҖүи§Ҷйў‘дёӯеҝғеҢәеҹҹ(зҷҪе№іиЎЎ)...")
        box = overlay.bounding_box()
        check("overlayжңүе°әеҜё", box is not None and box["width"] > 100,
              f"box={box['width'] if box else 0}x{box['height'] if box else 0}")

        if box:
            # д»ҺдёӯеҝғеҒҸе·ҰдёҠејҖе§Ӣ, жӢ–еҲ°еҸідёӢ 200x200
            cx = box["x"] + box["width"] / 2
            cy = box["y"] + box["height"] / 2
            x1 = cx - 100
            y1 = cy - 100
            x2 = cx + 100
            y2 = cy + 100

            page.mouse.move(x1, y1)
            time.sleep(0.2)
            page.mouse.down()
            time.sleep(0.1)
            page.mouse.move(x2, y2, steps=5)
            time.sleep(0.2)
            page.mouse.up()
            time.sleep(0.5)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 6: зӯүеҫ…зҷҪе№іиЎЎз»“жһң в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[6] зӯүеҫ…зҷҪе№іиЎЎз»“жһң Toast...")
        wb_success = False
        for i in range(15):
            time.sleep(1)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success, .toast-error, .toast-warning")
            if toast:
                text = toast.text_content()
                print(f"    Toast[{i}s]: {text}")
                if "зҷҪе№іиЎЎ" in text or "е·Іеә”з”Ё" in text:
                    wb_success = True
                    break
                if "еӨұиҙҘ" in text or "й”ҷиҜҜ" in text:
                    break
        check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎToastеҮәзҺ°", wb_success)

        # йӘҢиҜҒ overlay йҡҗи—Ҹ
        overlay_display2 = overlay.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).display")
        check("overlayе·Ійҡҗи—Ҹ", overlay_display2 == "none", f"display={overlay_display2}")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 7: зӮ№еҮ»еҜ№з„ҰжҢүй’® вҶ’ жЎҶйҖү в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[7] зӮ№еҮ»еҜ№з„ҰжҢүй’®...")
        btn_af = page.query_selector("#btnRegionAF")
        check("еҜ№з„ҰжҢүй’®еӯҳеңЁ", btn_af is not None)
        btn_af.click()
        time.sleep(1)

        overlay_display3 = overlay.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).display")
        check("overlayеҶҚж¬ЎжҳҫзӨә", overlay_display3 != "none")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 8: жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„ҰеҢәеҹҹ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[8] жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„ҰеҢәеҹҹ...")
        if box:
            x1 = cx - 100
            y1 = cy - 100
            x2 = cx + 100
            y2 = cy + 100
            page.mouse.move(x1, y1)
            time.sleep(0.2)
            page.mouse.down()
            time.sleep(0.1)
            page.mouse.move(x2, y2, steps=5)
            time.sleep(0.2)
            page.mouse.up()

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 9: зӯүеҫ…еҜ№з„Ұз»“жһң в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[9] зӯүеҫ…еҜ№з„Ұз»“жһң...")
        af_success = False
        for i in range(30):
            time.sleep(1)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success, .toast-error, .toast-warning, .toast-info")
            if toast:
                text = toast.text_content()
                print(f"    Toast[{i}s]: {text}")
                if "еҜ№з„Ұе®ҢжҲҗ" in text:
                    af_success = True
                    break
                if "еӨұиҙҘ" in text or "й”ҷиҜҜ" in text:
                    break
        check("еҜ№з„ҰToastеҮәзҺ°", af_success)

        # в”Җв”Җ жұҮжҖ» в”Җв”Җ
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"  з»“жһң: {passed} PASS / {failed} FAIL / {passed+failed} TOTAL")
        if failed == 0:
            print(f"  ALL PASS")
        else:
            print(f"  FAILURES DETECTED")
        print(f"{'='*60}")

        time.sleep(2)
        browser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

content.text: зҺ°еңЁеҶҷ E2E вҖ” е…ЁзЁӢзңҹе®һ UIпјҢеҗ«жЁЎејҸйҷҗеҲ¶йӘҢиҜҒгҖӮ

"""v8.10 E2E: Playwright зңҹе®һ UI жөӢиҜ•
1. иҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ вҶ’ 2. еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎжүӢеҠЁ вҶ’ 3. жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎ вҶ’ 4. еҲҮжҚўеҜ№з„ҰжүӢеҠЁ вҶ’ 5. жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„Ұ
6. йӘҢиҜҒиҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸдёӢжҢүй’®иў«жӢҰжҲӘ
"""
import time
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

URL = "http://127.0.0.1:10280"
passed = 0
failed = 0

def check(name, condition, detail=""):
    global passed, failed
    if condition:
        passed += 1
        print(f"  [PASS] {name}{' - ' + detail if detail else ''}")
    else:
        failed += 1
        print(f"  [FAIL] {name}: {detail}")

def run():
    global passed, failed
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=False)
        page = browser.new_page(viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080})
        page.on("dialog", lambda d: d.accept())

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 1: жү“ејҖйЎөйқў вҶ’ еҲҮжҚўеҲ°жҺ§еҲ¶еҸ° в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[1] жү“ејҖжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°...")
        page.goto(URL, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
        time.sleep(2)
        page.click('[data-page="console"]')
        time.sleep(1)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", state="visible", timeout=15000)
        check("жҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°еҸҜи§Ғ", True)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 2: йҖҡиҝҮ UI иҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[2] йҖҡиҝҮи®ҫеӨҮз®ЎзҗҶиҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ...")
        page.click('[data-page="devices"]')
        time.sleep(2)
        # зӯүи®ҫеӨҮеҲ—иЎЁеҲ·ж–°
        page.wait_for_selector('[id^="device-"]', timeout=10000)
        # жүҫејҖе…іжҢүй’®зӮ№еҮ»иҝһжҺҘ
        switches = page.query_selector_all('[id^="device-"] input[type="checkbox"]')
        check("и®ҫеӨҮејҖе…іеӯҳеңЁ", len(switches) > 0, f"{len(switches)} дёӘ")
        if switches:
            switch = switches[0]
            switch.click()
            time.sleep(2)
            # зӯүиҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒеҸҳеҢ–
            page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
        # еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°
        page.click('[data-page="console"]')
        time.sleep(2)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", state="visible", timeout=10000)
        check("еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°", True)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 3: йӘҢиҜҒиҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸдёӢзҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®иў«жӢҰжҲӘ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[3] йӘҢиҜҒиҮӘеҠЁзҷҪе№іиЎЎжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘ...")
        # е…Ҳзӯү WB жЁЎејҸеҠ иҪҪ
        time.sleep(3)
        wb_select = page.query_selector("#wbMode")
        check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎдёӢжӢүеӯҳеңЁ", wb_select is not None)
        if wb_select:
            wb_val = wb_select.input_value()
            print(f"    еҪ“еүҚзҷҪе№іиЎЎ: {wb_val}")
            if wb_val != "manual":
                # зӮ№еҮ»зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®еә”иў«жӢҰжҲӘ
                btn_wb = page.query_selector("#btnRegionWB")
                btn_wb.click()
                time.sleep(1)
                toast = page.query_selector(".toast-warning, .toast-error, .toast-info")
                toast_text = toast.text_content() if toast else ""
                check("иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘзҷҪе№іиЎЎ", "жүӢеҠЁ" in toast_text or "еҲҮжҚў" in toast_text,
                      f"toast={toast_text[:60]}")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 4: еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎдёәжүӢеҠЁ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[4] еҲҮжҚўзҷҪе№іиЎЎдёәжүӢеҠЁ...")
        if wb_select:
            page.select_option("#wbMode", "manual")
            time.sleep(2)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success")
            check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎжүӢеҠЁеҲҮжҚў", toast is not None,
                  toast.text_content()[:50] if toast else "ж— toast")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 5: жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[5] жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲҶжһҗ...")
        btn_wb = page.query_selector("#btnRegionWB")
        btn_wb.click()
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay = page.query_selector("#regionOverlay")
        overlay_vis = overlay.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).display")
        check("overlayжҳҫзӨә(зҷҪе№іиЎЎ)", overlay_vis != "none")

        # жӢ–жӢҪжЎҶйҖү
        box = overlay.bounding_box()
        if box and box["width"] > 100:
            cx = box["x"] + box["width"] / 2
            cy = box["y"] + box["height"] / 2
            x1, y1 = cx - 100, cy - 100
            x2, y2 = cx + 100, cy + 100
            page.mouse.move(x1, y1)
            page.mouse.down()
            page.mouse.move(x2, y2, steps=5)
            page.mouse.up()
            time.sleep(0.5)

        # зӯү toast
        wb_ok = False
        for i in range(20):
            time.sleep(1)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success, .toast-error")
            if toast:
                t = toast.text_content()
                print(f"    Toast[{i}s]: {t[:60]}")
                if "зҷҪе№іиЎЎ" in t or "е·Іеә”з”Ё" in t:
                    wb_ok = True; break
                if "еӨұиҙҘ" in t: break
        check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲҶжһҗе®ҢжҲҗ", wb_ok)

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 6: йӘҢиҜҒиҮӘеҠЁеҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸдёӢжӢҰжҲӘ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[6] йӘҢиҜҒиҮӘеҠЁеҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘ...")
        fm = page.query_selector("#focusMode")
        check("еҜ№з„ҰдёӢжӢүеӯҳеңЁ", fm is not None)
        if fm:
            fm_val = fm.input_value()
            print(f"    еҪ“еүҚеҜ№з„Ұ: {fm_val}")
            if fm_val != "manual":
                btn_af = page.query_selector("#btnRegionAF")
                btn_af.click()
                time.sleep(1)
                toast = page.query_selector(".toast-warning")
                toast_text = toast.text_content() if toast else ""
                check("иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘеҜ№з„Ұ", "жүӢеҠЁ" in toast_text or "еҲҮжҚў" in toast_text,
                      f"toast={toast_text[:60]}")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 7: еҲҮжҚўеҜ№з„ҰдёәжүӢеҠЁ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[7] еҲҮжҚўеҜ№з„ҰдёәжүӢеҠЁ...")
        if fm:
            page.select_option("#focusMode", "manual")
            time.sleep(2)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success")
            check("еҜ№з„ҰжүӢеҠЁеҲҮжҚў", toast is not None,
                  toast.text_content()[:50] if toast else "ж— toast")

        # в”Җв”Җ Step 8: жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„Ұ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[8] жЎҶйҖүеҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ...")
        btn_af = page.query_selector("#btnRegionAF")
        btn_af.click()
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis2 = overlay.evaluate("el => window.getComputedStyle(el).display")
        check("overlayжҳҫзӨә(еҜ№з„Ұ)", overlay_vis2 != "none")

        if box and box["width"] > 100:
            x1, y1 = cx - 100, cy - 100
            x2, y2 = cx + 100, cy + 100
            page.mouse.move(x1, y1)
            page.mouse.down()
            page.mouse.move(x2, y2, steps=5)
            page.mouse.up()
            time.sleep(0.5)

        af_ok = False
        for i in range(45):
            time.sleep(1)
            toast = page.query_selector(".toast-success, .toast-error, .toast-info")
            if toast:
                t = toast.text_content()
                print(f"    Toast[{i}s]: {t[:80]}")
                if "еҜ№з„Ұе®ҢжҲҗ" in t:
                    af_ok = True; break
                if "еӨұиҙҘ" in t or "й”ҷиҜҜ" in t: break
        check("еҜ№з„ҰеҲҶжһҗе®ҢжҲҗ", af_ok)

        # в”Җв”Җ жұҮжҖ» в”Җв”Җ
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"  {passed} PASS / {failed} FAIL / {passed+failed} TOTAL")
        if failed == 0:
            print(f"  ALL PASS")
        else:
            print(f"  FAILURES DETECTED")
        print(f"{'='*60}")
        time.sleep(2)
        browser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

# в”Җв”Җ Step 2: йҖҡиҝҮ UI иҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[2] йҖҡиҝҮи®ҫеӨҮз®ЎзҗҶиҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ...")
        page.click('[data-page="devices"]')
        time.sleep(2)
        # зӯүи®ҫеӨҮеҲ—иЎЁжёІжҹ“пјҲtoggleDeviceConnect ејҖе…іеңЁ deviceTable еҶ…пјү
        page.wait_for_selector("#deviceTable tr", timeout=10000)
        # жүҫејҖе…і checkboxпјҲonchange=toggleDeviceConnectпјү
        switches = page.query_selector_all('#deviceTable input[type="checkbox"]')
        check("и®ҫеӨҮејҖе…іеӯҳеңЁ", len(switches) > 0, f"{len(switches)} дёӘ")
        if switches:
            switch = switches[0]
            # еҰӮжһңжңӘеӢҫйҖүеҲҷзӮ№еҮ»иҝһжҺҘ
            is_checked = switch.is_checked()
            print(f"    ејҖе…іе·ІеӢҫйҖү: {is_checked}")
            if not is_checked:
                switch.click()
                time.sleep(3)
        # еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°
        page.click('[data-page="console"]')
        time.sleep(2)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", state="visible", timeout=10000)
        check("еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°", True)

"""v8.10 E2E: Playwright зңҹе®һ UI жөӢиҜ•
жөҒзЁӢ:
  A. зҷҪе№іиЎЎ: иҮӘеҠЁ(е®ҳж–№) вҶ’ йӘҢиҜҒжҢүй’®жӢҰжҲӘ вҶ’ еҲҮжүӢеҠЁ вҶ’ жЎҶйҖүеҲҶжһҗ
  B. еҜ№з„Ұ:   иҮӘеҠЁ вҶ’ йӘҢиҜҒжҢүй’®жӢҰжҲӘ вҶ’ еҲҮжүӢеҠЁ вҶ’ жЎҶйҖүеҲҶжһҗ
"""
import time
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

URL = "http://127.0.0.1:10280"
passed = 0
failed = 0

def check(name, condition, detail=""):
    global passed, failed
    if condition:
        passed += 1
        print(f"  [PASS] {name}{' - ' + detail if detail else ''}")
    else:
        failed += 1
        print(f"  [FAIL] {name}: {detail}")

def wait_toast(page, keyword, timeout_s=30):
    for i in range(timeout_s):
        time.sleep(1)
        for sel in [".toast-success", ".toast-error", ".toast-warning", ".toast-info"]:
            toast = page.query_selector(sel)
            if toast:
                t = toast.text_content() or ""
                if keyword in t:
                    return True, t
                if "еӨұиҙҘ" in t or "й”ҷиҜҜ" in t:
                    return False, t
    return False, "timeout"

def run():
    global passed, failed
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=False)
        page = browser.new_page(viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080})
        page.on("dialog", lambda d: d.accept())

        # в”Җв”Җ 1: жү“ејҖжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ° в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[1] жү“ејҖжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°...")
        page.goto(URL, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
        time.sleep(2)
        page.click('[data-page="console"]')
        time.sleep(1)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", state="visible", timeout=15000)
        check("жҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°еҸҜи§Ғ", True)

        # в”Җв”Җ 2: йҖҡиҝҮи®ҫеӨҮз®ЎзҗҶиҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ в”Җв”Җ
        print("\n[2] иҝһжҺҘи®ҫеӨҮ...")
        page.click('[data-page="devices"]')
        time.sleep(1)
        discover = page.query_selector("#btnDiscover")
        if discover:
            discover.click()
            time.sleep(5)
        page.wait_for_selector("#deviceTable tr", timeout=15000)
        switches = page.query_selector_all('#deviceTable input[type="checkbox"]')
        check("и®ҫеӨҮејҖе…іеӯҳеңЁ", len(switches) > 0, f"{len(switches)} дёӘ")
        if switches and not switches[0].is_checked():
            switches[0].click()
            time.sleep(3)
        page.click('[data-page="console"]')
        time.sleep(2)
        page.wait_for_selector("#btnConsoleSnapshot", state="visible", timeout=10000)
        check("еҲҮеӣһжҺ§еҲ¶еҸ°", True)

        # в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ A. зҷҪе№іиЎЎ в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
        print("\nв”Җв”Җ зҷҪе№іиЎЎжөӢиҜ• в”Җв”Җ")

        # A1: еҲҮзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲ°"иҮӘеҠЁпјҲе®ҳж–№пјү"
        print("\n[A1] еҲҮзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲ°иҮӘеҠЁ(е®ҳж–№)...")
        time.sleep(3)  # зӯүеҸӮж•°еҠ иҪҪ
        wb = page.query_selector("#wbMode")
        check("wbModeдёӢжӢүеӯҳеңЁ", wb is not None)
        if wb:
            page.select_option("#wbMode", "auto_official")
            time.sleep(2)
            current = wb.input_value()
            check("еҲҮеҲ°иҮӘеҠЁ(е®ҳж–№)", current == "auto_official", f"е®һйҷ…={current}")

        # A2: иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸдёӢзӮ№еҮ»зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®еә”иў«жӢҰжҲӘ
        print("\n[A2] иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸзӮ№еҮ»зҷҪе№іиЎЎжҢүй’®...")
        page.click("#btnRegionWB")
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis = page.evaluate("""() => {
            var el = document.getElementById('regionOverlay');
            return el ? window.getComputedStyle(el).display : 'none';
        }""")
        check("overlayжңӘжҳҫзӨә(иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘ)", overlay_vis == "none",
              f"display={overlay_vis}")
        # жЈҖжҹҘ toast жҸҗзӨә
        toast = page.query_selector(".toast-warning")
        if toast:
            t = toast.text_content() or ""
            check("жӢҰжҲӘжҸҗзӨәеҢ…еҗ«'жүӢеҠЁ'", "жүӢеҠЁ" in t, f"toast={t[:60]}")
        else:
            check("жңүжӢҰжҲӘtoast", False, "ж— toast")

        # A3: еҲҮеҲ°жүӢеҠЁжЁЎејҸ
        print("\n[A3] еҲҮзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲ°жүӢеӢ•...")
        page.select_option("#wbMode", "manual")
        ok, msg = wait_toast(page, "зҷҪе№іиЎЎ", 5)
        check("жүӢеҠЁеҲҮжҚўжҲҗеҠҹ", ok, msg[:60])

        # A4: жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲҶжһҗ
        print("\n[A4] жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲҶжһҗ...")
        page.click("#btnRegionWB")
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis = page.evaluate("""() => {
            var el = document.getElementById('regionOverlay');
            return el ? window.getComputedStyle(el).display : 'none';
        }""")
        check("overlayжҳҫзӨә(жүӢеҠЁжЁЎејҸ)", overlay_vis == "block", f"display={overlay_vis}")

        # жӢ–жӢҪжЎҶйҖү
        overlay_box = page.query_selector("#regionOverlay").bounding_box()
        if overlay_box and overlay_box["width"] > 100:
            cx = overlay_box["x"] + overlay_box["width"] / 2
            cy = overlay_box["y"] + overlay_box["height"] / 2
            # 200x200 жЎҶ
            page.mouse.move(cx - 100, cy - 100)
            page.mouse.down()
            page.mouse.move(cx + 100, cy - 100, steps=3)
            page.mouse.move(cx + 100, cy + 100, steps=3)
            page.mouse.up()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        else:
            check("overlayе°әеҜёжңүж•Ҳ", False, f"box={overlay_box}")

        ok, msg = wait_toast(page, "зҷҪе№іиЎЎ", 15)
        check("зҷҪе№іиЎЎеҲҶжһҗе®ҢжҲҗ", ok, msg[:80])

        # в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ B. еҜ№з„Ұ в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
        print("\nв”Җв”Җ еҜ№з„ҰжөӢиҜ• в”Җв”Җ")

        # B1: еҲҮеҜ№з„ҰеҲ°иҮӘеҠЁ
        print("\n[B1] еҲҮеҜ№з„ҰеҲ°иҮӘеҠЁ...")
        fm = page.query_selector("#focusMode")
        check("focusModeдёӢжӢүеӯҳеңЁ", fm is not None)
        if fm:
            page.select_option("#focusMode", "auto")
            time.sleep(2)
            current = fm.input_value()
            check("еҲҮеҲ°иҮӘеҠЁ", current == "auto", f"е®һйҷ…={current}")

        # B2: иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸзӮ№еҮ»еҜ№з„ҰжҢүй’®еә”иў«жӢҰжҲӘ
        print("\n[B2] иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸзӮ№еҮ»еҜ№з„ҰжҢүй’®...")
        page.click("#btnRegionAF")
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis = page.evaluate("""() => {
            var el = document.getElementById('regionOverlay');
            return el ? window.getComputedStyle(el).display : 'none';
        }""")
        check("overlayжңӘжҳҫзӨә(иҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸжӢҰжҲӘ)", overlay_vis == "none",
              f"display={overlay_vis}")
        toast = page.query_selector(".toast-warning")
        if toast:
            t = toast.text_content() or ""
            check("жӢҰжҲӘжҸҗзӨәеҢ…еҗ«'жүӢеҠЁ'", "жүӢеҠЁ" in t, f"toast={t[:60]}")
        else:
            check("жңүжӢҰжҲӘtoast", False, "ж— toast")

        # B3: еҚҠиҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸд№ҹеә”жӢҰжҲӘ
        print("\n[B3] еҚҠиҮӘеҠЁжЁЎејҸд№ҹеә”жӢҰжҲӘ...")
        page.select_option("#focusMode", "semiauto")
        time.sleep(1)
        page.click("#btnRegionAF")
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis = page.evaluate("""() => {
            var el = document.getElementById('regionOverlay');
            return el ? window.getComputedStyle(el).display : 'none';
        }""")
        check("еҚҠиҮӘеҠЁд№ҹжӢҰжҲӘ", overlay_vis == "none", f"display={overlay_vis}")

        # B4: еҲҮеҲ°жүӢеҠЁ
        print("\n[B4] еҲҮеҜ№з„ҰеҲ°жүӢеӢ•...")
        page.select_option("#focusMode", "manual")
        ok, msg = wait_toast(page, "еҜ№з„Ұ", 5)
        check("жүӢеҠЁеҲҮжҚўжҲҗеҠҹ", ok, msg[:60])

        # B5: жЎҶйҖүеҜ№з„ҰеҲҶжһҗ
        print("\n[B5] жЎҶйҖүеҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ...")
        page.click("#btnRegionAF")
        time.sleep(1)
        overlay_vis = page.evaluate("""() => {
            var el = document.getElementById('regionOverlay');
            return el ? window.getComputedStyle(el).display : 'none';
        }""")
        check("overlayжҳҫзӨә(жүӢеҠЁжЁЎејҸ)", overlay_vis == "block")

        if overlay_box and overlay_box["width"] > 100:
            page.mouse.move(cx - 100, cy - 100)
            page.mouse.down()
            page.mouse.move(cx + 100, cy - 100, steps=3)
            page.mouse.move(cx + 100, cy + 100, steps=3)
            page.mouse.up()
            time.sleep(0.5)

        ok, msg = wait_toast(page, "еҜ№з„Ұе®ҢжҲҗ", 60)
        check("еҜ№з„ҰеҲҶжһҗе®ҢжҲҗ", ok, msg[:80])

        # в”Җв”Җ жұҮжҖ» в”Җв”Җ
        total = passed + failed
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"  {passed} PASS / {failed} FAIL / {total} TOTAL")
        print(f"  {'ALL PASS' if failed == 0 else 'FAILURES DETECTED'}")
        print(f"{'='*60}")
        time.sleep(2)
        browser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

The actual logic is working вҖ” the intercept blocks correctly and everything else passes. Let me fix the toast detection to be more robust by listening for toast creation.

17/18, the one failure is that when clicking the AF button in auto mode, the toast captured is "еҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸ: auto" (the success toast from the previous mode switch) instead of the warning toast. The issue is that the toast from the mode switch is still being captured as a false positive. The toast "еҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸ: auto" is the result from `setFocusMode('auto')` which fires a "success" toast. 

The problem is that the focus mode change triggers a toast that includes "auto" in the success message, and that gets captured by the MutationObserver before the warning toast appears. Need to clear the captured toast after the mode switch toast arrives.

Let me fix the test: after B1 (switching to auto), wait for the focus mode toast and then clear it before B2.

The E2E tests are getting closer - 17/18 PASS on the last run. The only remaining FAIL is the toast interception check for focus mode. The issue is that when B1 switches focus to "auto", the toast from `setFocusMode` says "еҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸ: auto" which is a success toast, not a warning. Then when B2 clicks the focus button while in auto mode, our `toggleRegionMode` function checks `focusMode.value !== 'manual'` and shows a toast saying something about switching to manual mode. But the timing is tricky because the B1 toast is still around.

The last run showed: `toast='еҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸ: auto'` - this is the toast from B1 (switching to auto), not from B2 (clicking the focus button in auto mode).

The real issue is that B2's click on `#btnRegionAF` is supposed to be intercepted by our new code in `toggleRegionMode`. Let me check if the `focusMode` selector value is correct.

Actually, looking at the console.html, the `focusMode` select has options with values: "manual", "auto", "semiauto". And our toggleRegionMode checks `fm.value !== 'manual'`. So when we switch to "auto" in B1, the value is "auto", and when B2 clicks the focus button, it should be intercepted.

But the toast captured is "еҜ№з„ҰжЁЎејҸ: auto" which is the toast from setFocusMode in B1. The issue is the B1 toast hasn't disappeared when B2 runs. We added a 3s sleep in B1, but the toast lasts 3.7s (from the showToast code: setTimeout to remove after 3700ms). 

Let me fix the timing more carefully. Also, the toast from toggleRegionMode would say "еҜ№з„ҰйңҖеҲҮжҚўдёә"жүӢеҠЁ"жЁЎејҸжүҚиғҪдҪҝз”ЁеҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ" - so let's make the sleep longer and capture more specifically.

So the overlay IS being blocked (display=none), but the toast that was captured was from the B1 switch, not from the B2 click. The B2 toast should say "еҜ№з„ҰйңҖеҲҮжҚўдёә"жүӢеҠЁ"жЁЎејҸжүҚиғҪдҪҝз”ЁеҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ".

"""AstroHub v8.10 - жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹеҲҶжһҗ
зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈ (Gray World) + еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ (Laplacian зҲ¬еұұжҗңзҙў)
"""
import cv2
import numpy as np


def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """Gray World зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®—гҖӮ

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
    if g_mean < 1:
        g_mean = 1  # йҳІйҷӨйӣ¶
    red_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
    blue_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
    return {
        "red_gain": red_gain,
        "blue_gain": blue_gain,
        "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1),
        "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1),
        "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
    }


def calc_contrast(bgr: np.ndarray) -> float:

Let me analyze the current resource usage patterns:

1. **Import timing**: All `from src.vision.*` imports are inside functions (lazy), not at module level. This means they're only loaded when the endpoint is called.

2. **ContrastAF instance**: Created fresh each call, `af = ContrastAF(step_size=5, min_step=1)` вҖ” this is a small object (just 4 numbers), created and garbage collected after the function returns.

3. **StackEngine**: Created on `stack_start`, stored globally in `_stack_engine`, and cleared on `stack_stop`/`stack_cancel`. This is correct since it needs to persist across requests.

4. **Image decoding**: `_decode_image` creates numpy arrays, but they go out of scope when the function returns.

5. **focus_search**: The `bgr` array gets replaced each iteration, old ones GC'd.

Now let me think about what could be improved:

**Current issues:**
- `analyze_whitebalance` and `calc_contrast` use `cv2.mean()` and `cv2.Laplacian()` which are one-shot operations, fine
- `ContrastAF` is a tiny state machine, fine
- `focus_search` captures new frames via `client.capture_picture()` each iteration, the old bgr gets replaced
- No background threads, no persistent resources except StackEngine
- All vision imports are lazy (inside functions)

**What's fine:**
- Lazy imports вҖ” only load cv2/numpy when needed
- Stateless design for whitebalance
- Focus search releases resources when loop ends

**What could be a concern:**
- The `focus_search` loop is synchronous (blocking) вҖ” it runs in the FastAPI async handler but the heavy work (image decode, ISAPI calls) blocks the event loop
- The stack engine stores frames in memory (`self._stack_buffer`), could grow large

**Recommendations:**
1. Run `focus_search` in a thread pool executor to not block the async event loop
2. StackEngine already has `cancel()` to release buffer
3. Everything else is already stateless and lazy-loaded

def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®— (жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘/жӯ»зҷҪпјҢжҖ»еҖјеқҮиЎЎ)гҖӮ

    еҺҹзҗҶ:
    1. жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255) еғҸзҙ 
    2. зӣ®ж Ү: R_sum * red_gain = G_sum = B_sum * blue_gain
       вҶ’ red_gain = G_sum / R_sum * 100
       вҶ’ blue_gain = G_sum / B_sum * 100

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    # жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255)
    dead_black = np.all(bgr == 0, axis=2)
    dead_white = np.all(bgr == 255, axis=2)
    valid = ~(dead_black | dead_white)

    if np.count_nonzero(valid) < 10:
        # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ еӨӘе°‘пјҢеӣһйҖҖеҲ°е…ЁеұҖеқҮеҖј
        b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
        if g_mean < 1:
            g_mean = 1
        red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
        blue_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
        return {
            "red_gain": red_gain, "blue_gain": blue_gain,
            "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1), "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1), "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
        }

    # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ зҡ„ R/G/B жҖ»е’Ң
    b_ch = bgr[:, :, 0][valid]
    g_ch = bgr[:, :, 1][valid]
    r_ch = bgr[:, :, 2][valid]

    b_sum = float(b_ch.sum())
    g_sum = float(g_ch.sum())
    r_sum = float(r_ch.sum())

    # зӣ®ж Ү: R*gr = G = B*gb вҶ’ gr = G/R, gb = G/B
    if r_sum < 1:
        r_sum = 1
    if b_sum < 1:
        b_sum = 1

    red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_sum / r_sum * 100)))
    blue_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_sum / b_sum * 100)))

    r_mean = float(r_ch.mean())
    g_mean = float(g_ch.mean())
    b_mean = float(b_ch.mean())

    return {
        "red_gain": red_gain,
        "blue_gain": blue_gain,
        "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1),
        "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1),
        "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
    }

def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """Gray World зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®—гҖӮ

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
    if g_mean < 1:
        g_mean = 1  # йҳІйҷӨйӣ¶
    red_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
    blue_gain = max(0, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
    return {
        "red_gain": red_gain,
        "blue_gain": blue_gain,
        "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1),
        "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1),
        "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
    }

Looking at the current `focus_search` endpoint, it uses `ctrl.continuous_move(focus=speed)` which directly calls the PTZController object's internal method, not going through the same ISAPI path.

I need to check what `ptz_move` does for `focus-far`:

From the search result:
- `direction == "focus-near"` вҶ’ `ctrl.focus_move_continuous('near', speed)`
- There should also be a `focus-far` branch

Let me read more of ptz_move to see the focus-far handling.

I also need to use `mgr.ptz_stop(ip)` instead of `ctrl.stop_move()`.

The key issue is:
1. Replace `ctrl.continuous_move(focus=speed)` with `mgr.ptz_move(target_ip, direction='focus-far', speed=speed)`
2. Replace `ctrl.stop_move()` with `mgr.ptz_stop(target_ip)`

But wait - `mgr.ptz_move` takes speed as int, and we need it to work for focus. Looking at the code, `ptz_move` maps `focus-far` to `ctrl.focus_move_continuous('far', speed)`. This is the same path the UI uses.

content.text:
"""AstroHub v8.10 - жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹеҲҶжһҗ
зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈ (Gray World) + еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ (Laplacian зҲ¬еұұжҗңзҙў)
"""
import cv2
import numpy as np


def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®— (жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘/жӯ»зҷҪпјҢжҖ»еҖјеқҮиЎЎ)гҖӮ

    еҺҹзҗҶ:
    1. жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255) еғҸзҙ 
    2. зӣ®ж Ү: R_sum * red_gain = G_sum = B_sum * blue_gain
       вҶ’ red_gain = G_sum / R_sum * 100
       вҶ’ blue_gain = G_sum / B_sum * 100

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    # жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255)
    dead_black = np.all(bgr == 0, axis=2)
    dead_white = np.all(bgr == 255, axis=2)
    valid = ~(dead_black | dead_white)

    if np.count_nonzero(valid) < 10:
        # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ еӨӘе°‘пјҢеӣһйҖҖеҲ°е…ЁеұҖеқҮеҖј
        b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
        if g_mean < 1:
            g_mean = 1
        red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
        blue_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
        return {
            "red_gain": red_gain, "blue_gain": blue_gain,
            "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1), "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1), "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
        }

    # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ зҡ„ R/G/B жҖ»е’Ң
    b_ch = bgr[:, :, 0][valid]
    g_ch = bgr[:, :, 1][valid]
    r_ch = bgr[:, :, 2][valid]

    b_sum = float(b_ch.sum())
    g_sum = float(g_ch.sum())
    r_sum = float(r_ch.sum())

    # зӣ®ж Ү: R*gr = G = B*gb вҶ’ gr = G/R, gb = G/B
    if r_sum < 1:
        r_sum = 1
    if b_sum < 1:
        b_sum = 1

    red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_sum / r_sum * 100)))

The boss wants me to describe the white balance logic as a flow diagram. Let me trace the full flow from frontend to backend to ISAPI.

Frontend flow:
1. User clicks жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎ button вҶ’ overlay shows
2. User drags a region вҶ’ _finishRegion() called
3. _finishRegion captures canvas вҶ’ base64 вҶ’ POST /api/v1/vision/region-analyze
4. Response has red_gain, blue_gain вҶ’ POST /api/v1/ptz/{dev}/image/whitebalance with PUT XML

Backend flow:
1. router.py region_analyze endpoint
2. Calls analyze_whitebalance(bgr) вҶ’ gets red_gain, blue_gain
3. Returns to frontend

Then frontend calls setWhiteBalance(red_gain, blue_gain) вҶ’ PUT ISAPI

Let me trace the full end-to-end flow and present it as a logic diagram.

content.text:

content.thinking: иҖҒжқҝиҰҒжҲ‘зәҜж–Үеӯ—жҸҸиҝ°зҷҪе№іиЎЎзҡ„жЈҖжөӢе’ҢеўһзӣҠи®Ўз®—йҖ»иҫ‘пјҢдёҚиҰҒд»Јз ҒгҖӮ

content.text:
**жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎзҡ„жЈҖжөӢдёҺеўһзӣҠеҲӨж–ӯйҖ»иҫ‘пјҡ**

---

**дёҖгҖҒжҺ’йҷӨж— ж•ҲеғҸзҙ **

жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„еғҸзҙ жңүдёүз§ҚйңҖиҰҒжҺ’йҷӨпјҡ
1. **жӯ»й»‘** вҖ” R=G=B=0пјҢдј ж„ҹеҷЁеә•еҷӘжҲ–ж— е…үеҢәеҹҹпјҢдёҚиғҪеҸӮдёҺи®Ўз®—
2. **жӯ»зҷҪ** вҖ” R=G=B=255пјҢиҝҮжӣқжәўеҮәпјҢйҖҡйҒ“дҝЎжҒҜе·ІеӨұзңҹ
3. е…¶дҪҷеғҸзҙ йғҪжҳҜ"жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ "пјҢдҝқз•ҷеҸӮдёҺзҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈ

**дәҢгҖҒжЈҖжөӢжҳҜеҗҰйңҖиҰҒдҝ®жӯЈ**

д»Ҙ G йҖҡйҒ“дёәеҹәеҮҶпјҲдәәзңјеҜ№з»ҝиүІжңҖж•Ҹж„ҹпјҢдј ж„ҹеҷЁ G еғҸзҙ ж•°йҮҸд№ҹжҳҜ R/B зҡ„дёӨеҖҚпјүпјҡ

- и®Ўз®—жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ зҡ„ R жҖ»е’ҢгҖҒG жҖ»е’ҢгҖҒB жҖ»е’Ң
- еҰӮжһң RжҖ»е’Ң = GжҖ»е’Ң = BжҖ»е’ҢпјҢиҜҙжҳҺиҜҘеҢәеҹҹе·Із»ҸжҳҜдёӯжҖ§зҒ°/зҷҪпјҢдёҚйңҖиҰҒдҝ®жӯЈ
- еҰӮжһң RжҖ»е’Ң > GжҖ»е’ҢпјҢиҜҙжҳҺз”»йқўеҒҸзәўпјҢйңҖиҰҒйҷҚдҪҺ R еўһзӣҠ
- еҰӮжһң BжҖ»е’Ң > GжҖ»е’ҢпјҢиҜҙжҳҺз”»йқўеҒҸи“қпјҢйңҖиҰҒйҷҚдҪҺ B еўһзӣҠ
- еҸҚд№ӢдәҰз„¶

**дёүгҖҒеўһзӣҠеҖји®Ўз®—**

еҒҮи®ҫдёҖдёӘзҷҪиүІзү©дҪ“еңЁзҗҶжғізҷҪе№іиЎЎдёӢпјҢдёүдёӘйҖҡйҒ“зҡ„з§ҜеҲҶеҖјеә”иҜҘзӣёзӯүгҖӮеҪ“еүҚжЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ RжҖ»е’ҢгҖҒGжҖ»е’ҢгҖҒBжҖ»е’ҢеҲҶеҲ«д»ЈиЎЁдәҶеҪ“еүҚзҷҪе№іиЎЎи®ҫзҪ®дёӢзҡ„йҖҡйҒ“е“Қеә”ејәеәҰгҖӮ

```
и°ғж•ҙзӣ®ж Ү:  RжҖ»е’Ң Г— (ж–°RеўһзӣҠ/еҪ“еүҚRеўһзӣҠ) = GжҖ»е’Ң Г— (ж–°GеўһзӣҠ/еҪ“еүҚGеўһзӣҠ) = BжҖ»е’Ң Г— (ж–°BеўһзӣҠ/еҪ“еүҚBеўһзӣҠ)

д»Ҙ G дёәй”ҡпјҲGеўһзӣҠдёҚеҸҳпјү:
  RеўһзӣҠ = GжҖ»е’Ң / RжҖ»е’Ң Г— еҪ“еүҚRеўһзӣҠ
  BеўһзӣҠ = GжҖ»е’Ң / BжҖ»е’Ң Г— еҪ“еүҚBеўһзӣҠ
```

**еӣӣгҖҒеҗҲзҗҶжҖ§еҲӨж–ӯ**

- GжҖ»е’Ң / RжҖ»е’Ң е’Ң GжҖ»е’Ң / BжҖ»е’Ң зҡ„зү©зҗҶеҗ«д№үжҳҜ"RйҖҡйҒ“жҜ”GйҖҡйҒ“е·®еӨҡе°‘еҖҚ"пјҢз”ЁиҝҷдёӘжҜ”дҫӢзӣҙжҺҘдҪңдёәеўһзӣҠеҖј
- еўһзӣҠиҢғеӣҙй’ідҪҚеңЁ 1~255пјҲжө·еә· ISAPI е…Ғи®ёиҢғеӣҙпјү
- еҰӮжһңжңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ дёҚи¶і10дёӘпјҲжҜ”еҰӮжЎҶеҲ°зәҜй»‘еҢәеҹҹпјүпјҢдёҚдјҡи®Ўз®—еҮәжңүж„Ҹд№үзҡ„еўһзӣҠ вҶ’ еӣһйҖҖеҲ°е…ЁеұҖеқҮеҖјжі•е…ңеә•
- жһҒз«ҜеңәжҷҜпјҡжЎҶеҲ°дёҖзүҮзәҜзәў вҶ’ RжҖ»е’ҢиҝңеӨ§дәҺGжҖ»е’Ң вҶ’ RеўһзӣҠжҺҘиҝ‘1пјҲеҺӢзәўпјүвҶ’ BеўһзӣҠй«ҳпјҲжҸҗи“қпјүпјҢз»“жһңи¶Ӣеҗ‘дёӯжҖ§

**дә”гҖҒеҶҷе…Ҙи®ҫеӨҮ**

з®—еҮәзҡ„ R/B еўһзӣҠеҖјйҖҡиҝҮ ISAPI `PUT /Image/channels/1/whiteBalance` еҶҷе…Ҙи®ҫеӨҮпјҢи®ҫеӨҮз«ӢеҲ»з”ҹж•ҲгҖӮз”ЁжҲ·еҸҜд»ҘеңЁжЎҶйҖүеҗҺиӮүзңјеҲӨж–ӯз”»йқўжҳҜеҗҰеҒҸиүІж”№е–„гҖӮ

The boss is asking why the auto white balance always returns R=100, B=100. This is suspicious - it means the calculation is always coming out to exactly 100, which suggests either:

1. The area being analyzed has R=G=B (perfectly neutral), or
2. The calculation is wrong - maybe the gains are being set to a default/fixed value
3. The ISAPI write is using a fixed value instead of the calculated one

Let me trace through the actual code to find the bug. The frontend calls `_finishRegion` which sends base64 to `/api/v1/vision/region-analyze`, gets back red_gain/blue_gain, then calls `setWhiteBalance`. But wait - I need to check what the frontend actually does with the result.

Let me check the frontend code path and the API endpoint to trace what's actually happening.

So the frontend reads `r.whitebalance.red_gain` and `r.whitebalance.blue_gain`.

But the question is: why is it always returning R=100, B=100?

4. Converts to base64
5. Sends to `/api/v1/vision/region-analyze`

So the frontend IS cropping the image. The issue must be elsewhere.

```javascript

                })
```

Wait, `_finishRegionViaApi()` calls `/api/v1/ptz/{ip}/image/capture` which takes a full frame screenshot. Then the backend's `region_analyze` endpoint receives the full image with crop coordinates... but wait, does it actually crop?

3. еҗҺз«Ҝ `region_analyze` йңҖиҰҒж №жҚ®иҝҷдәӣеқҗж Үд»Һж•ҙеј еӣҫзүҮдёӯиЈҒеүӘеҮәжЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹ

The boss is asking how the backend knows which region the user selected when using the ISAPI capture path. Let me trace through the logic:

1. Frontend `_finishRegionViaApi()`:
   - Gets overlay dimensions (ow, oh)
   - Gets box position and size relative to overlay
   - Converts to ratios: sx=x/ow, sy=y/oh, sw=w/ow, sh=h/oh
   - Sends `{ image_path, x, y, w, h }` as ratios to backend

2. Backend `region_analyze()`:
   - If `image_path` is provided: reads the full image with cv2.imread
   - Then uses the ratios (req.x, req.y, req.w, req.h) to crop the image
   - x1 = x * image_width, y1 = y * image_height
   - x2 = (x + w) * image_width, y2 = (y + h) * image_height
   - bgr = bgr[y1:y2, x1:x2] вҶ’ the cropped region

So the answer is: the frontend sends the box coordinates as ratios (relative to the overlay size), and the backend crops the full ISAPI image using those ratios. The key assumption is that the overlay covers the same area as the ISAPI image.

The issue is: the overlay is over the WASM player area (#divPlugin), which has a 16:9 aspect ratio. The ISAPI full-resolution image might have a different aspect ratio than the WASM display area. The ratios might not map correctly.

Wait, the boss's question is simpler - he's asking "how do I know which part was selected". The answer is the ratio approach - frontend calculates ratios, backend crops.

But there's a potential issue: _finishRegionViaApi sends the ratios, but in the actual _finishRegion path (canvas path), it sends image_base64 directly (already cropped). So when canvas is available, it works differently.

The real question the boss is asking: if we switch to ISAPI path, how does the cropping work? The answer is via the x,y,w,h ratio parameters. The overlay covers the entire video area, the box position is relative to overlay, so the ratios should map correctly to any full-frame ISAPI capture.

The user is asking whether the focus search has the same problem - that the canvas capture produces blank frames, which would make the focus search invalid too.

Let me check the focus search code path. The `_finishRegion` function handles both whitebalance and focus modes. Both use `drawImage` on canvas to get the base64 image. If canvas produces black frames, then:

1. Whitebalance: gets R=100, B=100 (as confirmed)
2. Focus: gets black frames вҶ’ Laplacian contrast = 0 вҶ’ ContrastAF sees no contrast change вҶ’ gives up after a few steps

But wait, the E2E test showed focus was working (5 steps, contrast=72.95). Let me think about this...

Actually, the focus search endpoint (`/vision/focus-search`) doesn't just analyze the initial frame - it loops: capture вҶ’ analyze вҶ’ move вҶ’ ISAPI capture вҶ’ analyze вҶ’ move вҶ’ ...

In `focus_search`, after getting the initial `bgr` from the client, the loop does:
```python
# жҲӘеӣҫйӘҢиҜҒеҸҳеҢ–
try:
    jpg = client.capture_picture()
    if jpg:
        arr = np.frombuffer(jpg, np.uint8)
        bgr = cv2.imdecode(arr, cv2.IMREAD_COLOR)
```

So the initial frame comes from the canvas (which is likely black), but then the loop does ISAPI capture_picture() which gets real images from the device. So the focus search would:
- Step 0: analyze black frame вҶ’ contrast = 0
- Step 1: move focus, ISAPI capture вҶ’ real image вҶ’ contrast > 0
- Step 2: since contrast(real) > contrast(black)=0, it continues "дёҠеҚҮ"
- ...eventually finds peak

So focus works despite the initial black frame because the loop replaces it with ISAPI captures immediately. But the initial direction decision is based on a black frame comparison, which is wrong.

Actually wait, let me re-read: the focus_search in router.py gets the initial bgr from the client (frontend sends image_base64), then enters the loop where it takes ISAPI captures. So step 0 uses the canvas frame (black) вҶ’ contrast ~ 0, then step 1 uses ISAPI capture.

The key issue: both whitebalance and focus start with the canvas frame, but focus can recover because the endpoint uses ISAPI captures in the loop. Whitebalance uses the canvas frame and never gets real data.

So the user is right - both have the same root cause (canvas blank frame), but:
- Whitebalance: total failure (R=100 B=100)
- Focus: "works" but with a corrupted first step (starts from black frame contrast)

The fix is the same for both: use ISAPI capture instead of canvas.

The question is: should I confirm this and propose the unified fix?

content.text: еҲҶжһҗжё…жҘҡйңҖжұӮпјҢејҖе§Ӣе®һзҺ°гҖӮ

"""AstroHub v8.10 - жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹеҲҶжһҗ
зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈ (Gray World) + еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„Ұ (Laplacian зҲ¬еұұжҗңзҙў)
"""
import cv2
import numpy as np


def analyze_whitebalance(bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
    """зҷҪе№іиЎЎдҝ®жӯЈйҮҸи®Ўз®— (жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘/жӯ»зҷҪпјҢжҖ»еҖјеқҮиЎЎ)гҖӮ

    еҺҹзҗҶ:
    1. жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255) еғҸзҙ 
    2. зӣ®ж Ү: R_sum * red_gain = G_sum = B_sum * blue_gain
       вҶ’ red_gain = G_sum / R_sum * 100
       вҶ’ blue_gain = G_sum / B_sum * 100

    Args:
        bgr: жЎҶйҖүеҢәеҹҹзҡ„ BGR еӣҫеғҸ (numpy array)

    Returns:
        {"red_gain": int, "blue_gain": int, "r_mean": float, "g_mean": float, "b_mean": float}
    """
    # жҺ’йҷӨжӯ»й»‘ (0,0,0) е’Ңжӯ»зҷҪ (255,255,255)
    dead_black = np.all(bgr == 0, axis=2)
    dead_white = np.all(bgr == 255, axis=2)
    valid = ~(dead_black | dead_white)

    if np.count_nonzero(valid) < 10:
        # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ еӨӘе°‘пјҢеӣһйҖҖеҲ°е…ЁеұҖеқҮеҖј
        b_mean, g_mean, r_mean = cv2.mean(bgr)[:3]
        if g_mean < 1:
            g_mean = 1
        red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(r_mean, 1) * 100)))
        blue_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_mean / max(b_mean, 1) * 100)))
        return {
            "red_gain": red_gain, "blue_gain": blue_gain,
            "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1), "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1), "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
        }

    # жңүж•ҲеғҸзҙ зҡ„ R/G/B жҖ»е’Ң
    b_ch = bgr[:, :, 0][valid]
    g_ch = bgr[:, :, 1][valid]
    r_ch = bgr[:, :, 2][valid]

    b_sum = float(b_ch.sum())
    g_sum = float(g_ch.sum())
    r_sum = float(r_ch.sum())

    # зӣ®ж Ү: R*gr = G = B*gb вҶ’ gr = G/R, gb = G/B
    if r_sum < 1:
        r_sum = 1
    if b_sum < 1:
        b_sum = 1

    red_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_sum / r_sum * 100)))
    blue_gain = max(1, min(255, int(g_sum / b_sum * 100)))

    r_mean = float(r_ch.mean())
    g_mean = float(g_ch.mean())
    b_mean = float(b_ch.mean())

    return {
        "red_gain": red_gain,
        "blue_gain": blue_gain,
        "r_mean": round(r_mean, 1),
        "g_mean": round(g_mean, 1),
        "b_mean": round(b_mean, 1),
    }


def calc_contrast(bgr: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """и®Ўз®—еӣҫеғҸеҸҚе·®еҖј (Laplacian ж–№е·®)гҖӮ

    Args:
        bgr: BGR еӣҫеғҸ

    Returns:
        еҸҚе·®еҖјпјҢи¶Ҡй«ҳи¶Ҡжё…жҷ°
    """
    gray = cv2.cvtColor(bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
    return float(cv2.Laplacian(gray, cv2.CV_64F).var())


class ContrastAF:
    """еҸҚе·®еҜ№з„ҰзҲ¬еұұжҗңзҙўеҷЁгҖӮ

    з”Ёжі•:
        af = ContrastAF(client)
        # еҫӘзҺҜ:
        #   жҲӘеӣҫ вҶ’ af.step(bgr) вҶ’ иҝ”еӣһдёӢдёҖжӯҘжҢҮд»Ө
    """

    def __init__(self, step_size: int = 5, min_step: int = 1):
        self.step_size = step_size
        self.min_step = min_step
        self._prev_contrast = 0.0
        self._direction = 1      # 1=жӯЈеҗ‘(иҝңз„Ұ), -1=еҸҚеҗ‘(иҝ‘з„Ұ)
        self._peak_found = False
        self._reverse_count = 0   # еҸҚеҗ‘ж¬Ўж•°, и¶…иҝҮ2ж¬Ўи®ӨдёәжүҫеҲ°еі°еҖј

    def step(self, bgr: np.ndarray) -> dict:
        """иҫ“е…ҘеҪ“еүҚеё§пјҢиҝ”еӣһеҜ№з„Ұз§»еҠЁжҢҮд»ӨгҖӮ

        Returns:
            {"action": "focus_near"|"focus_far"|"stop", "contrast": float,
             "step": int, "message": str}
        """
        contrast = calc_contrast(bgr)

        if self._peak_found:
            return {"action": "stop", "contrast": round(contrast, 2),
                    "step": 0, "message": "еҜ№з„Ұе®ҢжҲҗ"}

        if self._prev_contrast == 0:
            # йҰ–ж¬Ў: жӯЈеҗ‘иҜ•жҺў
            self._prev_contrast = contrast
            return {"action": "focus_far", "contrast": round(contrast, 2),
                    "step": self.step_size, "message": "ејҖе§ӢеҜ№з„Ұжҗңзҙў"}

        if contrast > self._prev_contrast:
            # еҸҚе·®еңЁдёҠеҚҮ, з»§з»ӯеҗҢеҗ‘
            self._prev_contrast = contrast
            return {"action": "focus_far" if self._direction > 0 else "focus_near",
                    "contrast": round(contrast, 2),
                    "step": self.step_size, "message": "еҸҚе·®дёҠеҚҮ, з»§з»ӯ"}
        else:
            # еҸҚе·®дёӢйҷҚ, еҸҚеҗ‘
            self._reverse_count += 1
            self._direction *= -1
            self.step_size = max(self.min_step, self.step_size // 2)
            self._prev_contrast = contrast

            if self._reverse_count >= 3 or self.step_size <= self.min_step:
                self._peak_found = True
                return {"action": "stop", "contrast": round(contrast, 2),
                        "step": 0, "message": "еҜ№з„Ұе®ҢжҲҗ"}

            return {"action": "focus_far" if self._direction > 0 else "focus_near",
                    "contrast": round(contrast, 2),
                    "step": self.step_size, "message": "еҸҚе·®дёӢйҷҚ, еҸҚеҗ‘зІҫи°ғ"}

```
    read current_R, current_B from device
    
    delta_R = target_R - current_R
    delta_B = target_B - current_B
    
    # Adjust by at most step_size
    new_R = current_R + clamp(delta_R, -step_size, +step_size)
    new_B = current_B + clamp(delta_B, -step_size, +step_size)
    
    write to device
    
    # Re-capture to measure effect
    capture вҶ’ crop вҶ’ measure R/G/B means
    log result
```

This makes sense. Each iteration:
1. Captures a fresh image (reflecting previous adjustment)
2. Analyzes to find ideal gain
3. Adjusts by at most current step_size
4. Writes to device
5. Re-captures to verify

```python
class WhiteBalanceIterator:
    """иҝӯд»ЈзҷҪе№іиЎЎжҗңзҙўеҷЁгҖӮйҖҗжӯҘйҖјиҝ‘жңҖдҪі R/B еўһзӣҠгҖӮ"""
    
    def __init__(self, step_sizes=[5, 2, 1]):
        self.step_sizes = step_sizes
        self.step_index = 0
        
    def iterate(self, bgr, current_r, current_b):
        """дёҖж¬Ўиҝӯд»ЈгҖӮ
        Returns: (new_r, new_b, done, log_msg)
        """
        wb = analyze_whitebalance(bgr)
        target_r = wb['red_gain']
        target_b = wb['blue_gain']
        
        if self.step_index >= len(self.step_sizes):
            return current_r, current_b, True, "зҷҪе№іиЎЎе·Іе®ҢжҲҗпјҢж— йңҖи°ғж•ҙ"
        
        step = self.step_sizes[self.step_index]
        delta_r = target_r - current_r
        delta_b = target_b - current_b
        
        # Clamp to step size
        adj_r = max(-step, min(step, delta_r))
        adj_b = max(-step, min(step, delta_b))
        
        if adj_r == 0 and adj_b == 0:
            self.step_index += 1
            if self.step_index >= len(self.step_sizes):
                return current_r, current_b, True, "зҷҪе№іиЎЎж”¶ж•ӣе®ҢжҲҗ"
            # Try next smaller step
            step = self.step_sizes[self.step_index]
            delta_r = target_r - current_r
            delta_b = target_b - current_b
            adj_r = max(-step, min(step, delta_r))
            adj_b = max(-step, min(step, delta_b))
            if adj_r == 0 and adj_b == 0:
                self.step_index += 1
                if self.step_index >= len(self.step_sizes):
                    return current_r, current_b, True, "зҷҪе№іиЎЎж”¶ж•ӣе®ҢжҲҗ"
        
        new_r = max(1, min(255, current_r + adj_r))
        new_b = max(1, min(255, current_b + adj_b))
        
        self.step_index += 1
        if self.step_index >= len(self.step_sizes):
            return new_r, new_b, True, f"жңҖеҗҺи°ғж•ҙ (жӯҘй•ҝ{step})"
        
        return new_r, new_b, False, f"и°ғж•ҙжӯҘй•ҝ{step}"
```

```python
@api_router.post("/vision/whitebalance-iterate", ...)
async def whitebalance_iterate(data: dict) -> dict:
    """иҝӯд»ЈзҷҪе№іиЎЎгҖӮ"""
    device_id = data["device_id"]
    x, y, w, h = data["x"], data["y"], data["w"], data["h"]
    
    mgr, client, target_ip = ...
    
    logs = []
    logs.append({"level": "info", "msg": f"жЎҶйҖүзҷҪе№іиЎЎејҖе§ӢпјҢеҢәеҹҹX={x*100:.0f}%~{(x+w)*100:.0f}%пјҢY={y*100:.0f}%~{(y+h)*100:.0f}%"})
    
    # Read current WB values
    current_r, current_b = read_wb_from_device(client)
    
    for step_size in [5, 2, 1]:
        # Capture
        jpg = client.capture_picture()
        save_temp(jpg, "WB")
        bgr = cv2.imdecode(...)
        # Crop
        h_img, w_img = bgr.shape[:2]
        x1, y1 = int(x*w_img), int(y*h_img)
        x2, y2 = int((x+w)*w_img), int((y+h)*h_img)
        region = bgr[y1:y2, x1:x2]
        
        # Analyze
        wb = analyze_whitebalance(region)
        target_r, target_b = wb['red_gain'], wb['blue_gain']
        
        # Adjust
        delta_r = target_r - current_r
        delta_b = target_b - current_b
        adj_r = max(-step_size, min(step_size, delta_r))
        adj_b = max(-step_size, min(step_size, delta_b))
        
        if adj_r == 0 and adj_b == 0:
            logs.append({"level": "info", "msg": f"жӯҘй•ҝ{step_size}пјҡе·Іж”¶ж•ӣпјҢи·іиҝҮ"})
            continue
        
        new_r = max(1, min(255, current_r + adj_r))
        new_b = max(1, min(255, current_b + adj_b))
        
        # Write
        write_wb_to_device(client, new_r, new_b)
        current_r, current_b = new_r, new_b
        
        # Verify
        time.sleep(0.3)
        jpg = client.capture_picture()
        bgr = cv2.imdecode(...)
        region = bgr[y1:y2, x1:x2]
        r_mean, g_mean, b_mean = cv2.mean(region)[:3]
        
        logs.append({
            "level": "info",
            "msg": f"и°ғж•ҙзҷҪе№іиЎЎ(жӯҘй•ҝ{step_size})пјҡи®ҫзҪ®R={new_r} B={new_b}пјҢз»“жһңR={r_mean:.0f} G={g_mean:.0f} B={b_mean:.0f}"
        })
    
    logs.append({"level": "success", "msg": "зҷҪе№іиЎЎе®ҢжҲҗпјҢжү§иЎҢжё…зҗҶ..."})
    cleanup_temp("WB")
    logs.append({"level": "info", "msg": "жё…зҗҶе®ҢжҲҗ"})
    
    return {"success": True, "final_r": current_r, "final_b": current_b, "log": logs}
```

For the temp directory management, I need a helper:
```python
def _save_temp_image(jpg_bytes, category, device_id):
    temp_dir = Path("astrohub/temp") / category
    temp_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S_%f")
    fname = f"{device_id.replace('.', '_')}_{ts}.jpg"
    path = temp_dir / fname
    path.write_bytes(jpg_bytes)
    return str(path)

def _cleanup_temp(category):
    temp_dir = Path("astrohub/temp") / category
    if temp_dir.exists():
        for f in temp_dir.iterdir():
            f.unlink()
```

Actually, this is getting complex. Let me re-think the WB iteration logic more carefully.

The goal is: R_sum = G_sum = B_sum after correction. 

Current state: we have R_sum, G_sum, B_sum from a cropped region.
The current R/B gain values applied to the device are R_cur, B_cur (read from ISAPI).

Desired: R_sum Г— R_target = G_sum Г— 100 = B_sum Г— B_target (G stays at 100 as reference)

Actually, the gain values are relative. ISAPI WhiteBalanceRed and WhiteBalanceBlue are gain values where 100 = neutral.

After applying gain values (R_gain, B_gain), the relationship is:
- Device multiplies red sensor output by R_gain/100
- Device multiplies blue sensor output by B_gain/100
- Green keeps at 100/100 = 1.0

So if image has R_sum_raw, and gain is R_gain, the observed R_sum = R_sum_raw Г— (R_gain/100).

To make R_sum = G_sum = B_sum:
- We need R_sum Г— (R_new / R_current) = G_sum = B_sum Г— (B_new / B_current)
  (where R_new/B_new are the new gain values to apply)

But actually we don't know R_current and B_current easily. Let me think...

Actually, we can just iteratively approach:
- If R_sum > G_sum: decrease R by step вҶ’ red too strong, reduce
- If R_sum < G_sum: increase R by step вҶ’ red too weak, increase  
- Same for B

The iterative approach:
1. Read current R_gain, B_gain from device
2. Capture вҶ’ crop вҶ’ compute R_sum, G_sum, B_sum
3. For step size s (5, 3, 2, 1):
   a. Try adjusting: 
      - if R_sum > G_sum: R' = R_gain - s
      - if R_sum < G_sum: R' = R_gain + s
      - if B_sum > G_sum: B' = B_gain - s
      - if B_sum < G_sum: B' = B_gain + s
   b. Write R', B' to device
   c. Capture вҶ’ crop вҶ’ compute new R_sum', G_sum', B_sum'
   d. Compute error: |R'/G' - 1| + |B'/G' - 1|
   e. If error improved: keep changes, R_gain = R', B_gain = B'
   f. If error worse: revert, try opposite direction
   g. If both directions worse: move to next step size
4. Repeat with smaller step until step=1

```python
    jpg = client.capture_picture()
    if not jpg: return None
    ts = int(time.time() * 1000)
    img_path = os.path.join(temp_dir, f"capture_{ts}.jpg")
    with open(img_path, 'wb') as f: f.write(jpg)
    bgr = cv2.imdecode(np.frombuffer(jpg, np.uint8), cv2.IMREAD_COLOR)
    isapi_h, isapi_w = bgr.shape[:2]
    # crop
    x1 = max(0, int(x * isapi_w))
    y1 = max(0, int(y * isapi_h))
    x2 = min(isapi_w, int((x + w) * isapi_w))
    y2 = min(isapi_h, int((y + h) * isapi_h))
    cropped = bgr[y1:y2, x1:x2] if x2 > x1 and y2 > y1 else bgr
    return cropped, img_path, isapi_w, isapi_h, x1, y1, x2, y2
```
